hypothesis: Incorporating temporal degradation dynamics into foundation model pre-training by augmenting SMILES corpora with sequential electrochemical cycling data will enable FM embeddings to encode kinetic stability features that static molecular representations cannot capture, such that a single FM-derived formulation descriptor will simultaneously predict both initial performance metrics and capacity fade trajectories across at least 500 cycles with lower MAE than models trained on composition-only representations, with the hypothesis being falsifiable by comparing cycle-life prediction accuracy between temporally-augmented and standard SMILES-pretrained models on a held-out dataset of electrolytes with known degradation profiles.
